item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry in advance for the wall of text.

I feel like this one has so much unrealized potential that I dusted off my old as heck unused account to post this… IMO this SCP takes some half-steps in a couple of really good directions but doesn’t end up going much of anywhere.

This is all just my subjective opinion of course. But I think the scaled-up repetition of the behavior is just not really that compelling by itself. And there are some neat properties that are quite spooky if you think about them. But it’s a bit hard to take seriously in its present form.

These are the two directions I can think of. They are pretty incompatible with one another. And ultimately I’m a total newb when it comes to writing/editing these and it could be too similar to a different SCP or whatever. Both of these changes would definitely take the SCP far out of the Safe zone. They could potentially be worked in as an evolution to the anomaly, rather than an article retcon.

+ show block
– hide block

1. Unbounded Size Growth
The story is mostly the same, but with there being so much metal under the Earth’s crust, it seems like this SCP could assimilate the whole planet if left unchecked. Every time the Wire Figure makes 101 copies at its current size (102 total), they consolidate into a larger core figure (not stopping at the first growth increase). The original SCP-068 figure is always at the innermost core, so you could always interrupt this process by extracting and recontaining it. The theoretical endgame would be these things at a planetary scale, digging through continental plates and destroying the planet.

2. Unbounded Numerical Growth
This would be my personal favorite option. SCP-068 always stays small. Whenever they hit a count of 102, the original instance performs a ritual that permanently imbues all the other figures with all core properties of the SCP. Each instance goes out to create 101 more instances in the same manner and repeating the same ritual. (Note: The numbers involved in this process are aesthetically kinda cool. 1, 102, 10302, 1040502, 105090702, 10614160902…)

This is a typical exponential-growth nanobot-style situation. But I think there’s a bit of grisly potential here. What if someone has metal inside of them? Shrapnel… Jaw work… A new hip… A pacemaker… Being swarmed by these little guys and scratched to death as they burrow deep inside you to extract your metal would be horrifying. What if they were able to slowly collect the iron in your blood? Even if you didn’t have any solid metal per se, you’d probably live for way too long totally covered in a bath of 068-N instances meticulously scratching out all your blood cells. This goes for any other form of life that would internally use metals in any way. (All of them?) They’d still be able to threaten the planet itself quite easily, as well.

Even with the cumbersome requirement of initial activation via electrical current, the risk of a runaway process (and the torturous billion-scratch deaths) could raise the level of respect that this humble wire figure receives.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image is owned by author. As such, it is automatically released under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, what would this do with Mercury? Would it try to make figures out of it, or realize it's impossible and move on?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Alexus-0, while it's great that you seem to be endeavoring to comment on every SCP article you read, it might be good to slow down a bit with your posting so you can use proper grammar and capitalization. People will be more likely to respond to a comment that looks thoughtfully-written rather than rushed out in a hurry, and also it'll be less likely to be interpreted as spam.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the little guys the mecha feels a bit weird. the whole thing screams broken god but that might just be me seeing too much, if not then i feel it needs to be made a little clearer. something about church activity trying to steal it or speaking in techno-verse once it gets big enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whit so how long does the current have to be passed ? Just once and it's active? Or continuous? And the little guys don't need current? I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That picture is awful. Is there an in-universe justification for being blurry?

Otherwise, great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What intrigues me the most is how much this relatively minor little anomaly just screams "Broken God component!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed! Very much a Series I document, but an intriguing anomaly that can be either manageable or not, depending.

…Although let's not let this be anywhere near SCP-2844.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Articles like these were how I got into the site, ultimately. This one holds up really well still.

item.title: A small edit?
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed the SCP overall, a small edit I believe should be made the use of the word "union" is better suited than the word "unison" in the phrase "Gamma, beta, and theta waves begin emanating from SCP-068 after this unison." This describes all 102 figures coming together into their single, larger form, not their cooperation, hence not "In unison."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really like this. It's the perfect amount of mystery and intrigue; these guys are building a miniature army of wire stickmen… but why? It's the openness to interpretation and possibilities this could open that I adore, and the imagery of a corps consisting of tiny stickmen is very charming and unsettling. I like to imagine these guys are at least partly related to the Broken God in some way. Maybe his militia?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed; thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "tear though anything" <through

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wiki newbie messed up, realized too late that she can edit out typos herself, and can't delete her unnecessary typo correction comment - sorry about that, nothing more to see here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really like the wire mecha thing. Something about this has a real "Eric" feel to it, possibly because I was just reading some of his other stuff. Upvoted despite the end note being a bit "lolfoundation", but I do agree that it should be Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should probably do away with the part about the wire figures combining into a SUPER WIRE DUDE thing. The idea of a wire stick figure that tries to build more of itself is neat, but I think you go into way too much detail with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually one of my favorites. I find it so charming, even if the tone could be better and the addenda suck. It might just be nostalgia, since this is one of the first I read and liked, but whatever. It's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the actual name for Teflon is Polytetrafluoroethylene

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's cool, but the execution is a little sloppy.

forming themselves into as big a figure as possible.

doesn't roll out well. Maybe 'Large' would work better in this case. I'm picky.

after this unison.

shouldn't that be 'after this union'?

While 068 will not attempt to reach metals that are too difficult to get to, it will tear though anything that is soft enough for its limbs to penetrate. What it considers "soft enough" changes depending on what 068 is shaped from at the time.

These two sentences just… they hit me wrong and I don't know why. They don't seem as clinical as the rest of the piece, maybe?

I like the idea, but some of these errors are preventing me from voting it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really can't upvote this with the addendums in place. Ugh. Besides that, this is kind of neat.

EDIT: Ultimately downvoted for the Wacey stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch. I upped it to 9.8 centimeters, because A) That makes it 20 figures tall, giving plenty of material for the arms, legs, and head, and B) It looks about that tall anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a basic error in the maximum size of the large figure formed when multiples come together. If each individual wire man is 1.8 cm tall, then placing 102 of them end-to-end makes a line only 1.836 m long, not the two meters described in the article. Even if we allow for rounding to two meters, we do not then have a larger figure, complete with limbs and a head; rather, we have a teetering column of 102 tiny figures standing atop each other, producing simultaneously both the world's shortest human ladder and the one with the most members.

The size of either the large or small figure should be adjusted or the maximum number of small figures should be increased.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one though the stage 2 idea seems a bit half baked. I can see the dangers if activated but the fact that it can be stuffed into a locker and forgotten about, deems it worthing of a "Safe" rating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally think this is kinda cute. The two addendums and the document can go, but this amuses me in just the right way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really does not move me emotionally. I agree with pooryoric.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of the Broken God being a Stick Figure made me giggle, sorry. But that is a cool idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could this be another piece of the Broken God? His skeleton or another part of his body?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Wacey, what would the figure do if it has enough metall to create 102 copies at stage 2?
Does it need electric current for all time or only once, for activation? If so, can it be activated by statical discharge from careless personnel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty really meh about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reproducing imperfect copies, I like. The giant mecha, not so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Designation error.

SCP-068-01 would refer to a very specific object that we're keeping as part of the SCP, not merely to any theoretical objects that could be created by the SCP. At least, that's what I feel precedent dictates.

Not much of an error, but it's my job to be SIR NITPICKSALOT.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Hmmm, I like the idea of the metal stick figure becoming animated, but I think the part about it making a replica of itself over and over again is stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be interesting to see how this thing would react to SCP-882. Both of them are metal SCPs that manipulate metal regardless of properties. Hrm…

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
I see your point. But if it is used, then what are you going to do? You'd have who knows how many indestructible figures laying about with some very abnormal properties.
Also, you have to wait for the current to wear out. And that only happens 4m32s after they become 068-1. Then you would end up with a giant indestructible wire figure. How would you extract 068 from all of that?

But if you could come up with a way to use SCP-068 with out any of the aforementioned problems then, by all means let 'er rip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stick-figure nanites!

Actually, isn't there something to the idea of using it to destroy metal SCPs? The stick figures are only animate when a current flows through them, right?

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: I had actually though of that but didn't bother mentioning it. It would make copies from the tele-kill that would be tele-proof. Like I said, all of the source metals qualities are retained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any thought on what would happen when this SCP is introduced to a sample of the Tele-kill alloy (I forget its SCP # at the moment)?
